opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: It feels like there is a shard of glass scraping my eye every time I blink, and everything is blurry.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Sudden severe eye pain and blurry vision after getting something in my eye while sanding wood a couple of hours ago.

symptom_narrative.onset: abruptly, about 2 hours ago, while sanding wood without safety goggles
symptom_narrative.duration: constant and worsening since it started
symptom_narrative.character: started gritty, now sharp and stabbing, like glass scraping with every blink
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: keeping my eyes closed and the lights off
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: watering non-stop, can barely keep the eye open, vision in that eye is blurry
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: can't see well out of that eye, can't tolerate any light, roommate is helping me get around

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: was doing DIY woodworking without eye protection; roommate present and able to help

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: worried about permanent vision loss, wants to know if this can wait until morning

communication_style.typical_message_style: short messages, keeping eyes closed makes typing difficult, roommate may relay some details
